en_definition: sad because something is not as good as expected
example1: I was disappointed with the movie last night.
example1_ru: Вчера вечером я разочаровался в этом фильме.
example2: She felt disappointed when her plan fell through.

en_definition: feeling sure about your ability to do something
example1: He is confident in his English speaking skills.
example1_ru: Он уверен в своих навыках разговорного английского.
example2: I felt more confident after practicing the presentation.

en_definition: able to wait calmly without complaining
example1: A good teacher must be patient with students.
example1_ru: Хороший учитель должен быть терпелив со студентами.
example2: You should be patient while learning new skills.

en_definition: able to be trusted to do what you promise
example1: My friend is very reliable and always helps me.
example1_ru: Мой друг очень надёжный и всегда мне помогает.
example2: We need a reliable person for this project.

en_definition: feeling shy or ashamed in front of others
example1: She was embarrassed when everyone looked at her.
example1_ru: Она смутилась, когда все на неё посмотрели.
example2: I felt embarrassed after making that silly mistake.

en_definition: slightly angry or irritated
example1: He was annoyed by the constant noise.
example1_ru: Его раздражал постоянный шум.
example2: My sister gets annoyed when I borrow her things.

en_definition: willing to give money or help freely
example1: My uncle is generous and always gives gifts.
example1_ru: Мой дядя щедрый и всегда дарит подарки.
example2: She made a generous donation to the charity.

en_definition: caring only about yourself and not others
example1: It was selfish of him to take all the food.
example1_ru: С его стороны было эгоистично забрать всю еду.
example2: She left without saying goodbye selfish.

en_definition: not afraid to do dangerous or difficult things
example1: The brave soldier helped people during danger.
example1_ru: Храбрый солдат помогал людям в опасности.
example2: It was brave of you to speak in public.

en_definition: happy and positive in mood
example1: She has a cheerful personality and smiles often.
example1_ru: У неё жизнерадостный характер, и она часто улыбается.
example2: The cheerful music made everyone dance.

en_definition: feeling worried or afraid about something
example1: I felt nervous before my exam today.
example1_ru: Сегодня перед экзаменом я нервничал.
example2: She gets nervous when meeting new people.

en_definition: not proud about your achievements
example1: Despite his success he remains humble.
example1_ru: Несмотря на успех, он остаётся скромным.
example2: A humble person does not boast about things.

en_definition: to reserve or arrange something in advance
example1: I will book a hotel for my vacation.
example1_ru: Я забронирую отель для своего отпуска.
example2: We need to book tickets for the flight.

en_definition: to decide something will not happen
example1: The game was cancelled due to bad weather.
example1_ru: Матч отменили из-за плохой погоды.
example2: I had to cancel my appointment yesterday.

en_definition: to delay something to a later time
example1: We decided to postpone the meeting.
example1_ru: Мы решили перенести встречу.
example2: The concert was postponed until next month.

en_definition: a rectangular case used for carrying clothes and belongings when traveling
example1: She packed her suitcase the night before the flight.
example1_ru: Она собрала чемодан накануне вечером перед вылетом.
example2: His suitcase was too heavy to lift onto the shelf.

en_definition: official permission to enter or stay in a country
example1: I need to get a visa before traveling.
example1_ru: Мне нужно получить визу перед поездкой.
example2: The visa will expire next month.

en_definition: official document showing your identity and nationality
example1: My passport expires in two years.
example1_ru: Срок действия моего паспорта истекает через два года.
example2: You need a valid passport to fly abroad.

en_definition: in or to a foreign country
example1: I want to study abroad next year.
example1_ru: В следующем году я хочу учиться за границей.
example2: My family went abroad for vacation.

en_definition: a place where you can stay overnight
example1: We found good accommodation near the city.
example1_ru: Мы нашли хорошее жильё недалеко от центра города.
example2: The hotel provides comfortable accommodation.

en_definition: to get onto an airplane in order to begin a flight
example1: Passengers began to board a plane an hour before departure.
example1_ru: Пассажиры начали посадку в самолёт за час до вылета.
example2: We must board a plane quickly once the gate opens.

en_definition: to leave a hotel after paying the bill
example1: We have to check out by 11am tomorrow.
example1_ru: Завтра нам нужно освободить номер до 11 утра.
example2: They checked out early this morning.

en_definition: to start a journey to a destination
example1: We set off for the mountains early.
example1_ru: Мы отправились в горы рано утром.
example2: They set off on their adventure yesterday.

en_definition: to want something to happen in the future
example1: I look forward to visiting Paris next summer.
example1_ru: Я с нетерпением жду поездки в Париж следующим летом.
example2: She looks forward to the weekend.

en_definition: making you feel interested and enthusiastic
example1: The exciting news made her jump.
example1_ru: Волнующая новость заставила её подпрыгнуть от радости.
example2: We had an exciting adventure last week.

en_definition: not interested because something is dull
example1: I got bored watching the slow movie.
example1_ru: Мне стало скучно смотреть этот медленный фильм.
example2: The boring lecture made everyone yawn.

en_definition: pleased about your achievements or family
example1: She was proud of her excellent grades.
example1_ru: Она гордилась своими отличными оценками.
example2: We are proud to be part of this team.

en_definition: unhappy because someone has something you want
example1: He felt jealous when she got promoted.
example1_ru: Он почувствовал ревность, когда её повысили.
example2: Do not be jealous of other people.

en_definition: feeling thankful for something you received
example1: I am grateful for your help today.
example1_ru: Я благодарен тебе за сегодняшнюю помощь.
example2: She was grateful when he helped her.

en_definition: always telling the truth and not cheating
example1: He is an honest person who never lies.
example1_ru: Он честный человек, который никогда не лжёт.
example2: Be honest with yourself about mistakes.

en_definition: not wanting to work or do anything
example1: He was too lazy to do his homework.
example1_ru: Ему было слишком лень делать домашнее задание.
example2: Lazy workers do not achieve success.

en_definition: making a lot of effort in your work
example1: Hardworking students usually get good grades.
example1_ru: Трудолюбивые студенты обычно получают хорошие оценки.
example2: She is a hardworking employee.

en_definition: very firm about rules and punishing disobedience
example1: My strict teacher does not accept late work.
example1_ru: Мой строгий учитель не принимает работы с опозданием.
example2: Strict parents have rules for their children.

en_definition: able to change or adapt easily
example1: A flexible schedule works for everyone.
example1_ru: Гибкий график удобен для всех.
example2: We need flexible people on our team.

en_definition: to become happier, or to help someone feel happier
example1: Her friends tried to cheer up her after the bad news.
example1_ru: Друзья пытались подбодрить её после плохих новостей.
example2: A short walk outside can cheer up almost anyone.

en_definition: to agree to something you don't want
example1: I eventually gave in to peer pressure.
example1_ru: В итоге я поддался давлению сверстников.
example2: She finally gave in and bought the shoes.

en_definition: to accept an unpleasant situation or person without complaining
example1: I cannot put up with such rude behavior any longer.
example1_ru: Я больше не могу мириться с таким грубым поведением.
example2: She had to put up with the noise from construction next door.

en_definition: to raise a child or mention a topic
example1: Her parents brought her up in London.
example1_ru: Родители воспитали её в Лондоне.
example2: I want to bring up an important issue.

en_definition: activity that involves physical exercise and rules
example1: Football is my favorite sport to play.
example1_ru: Футбол — мой любимый вид спорта.
example2: Many sports require good physical fitness.

en_definition: person who trains people in sports
example1: Our coach teaches us basketball techniques.
example1_ru: Наш тренер обучает нас баскетбольной технике.
example2: The coach is very strict about training.

en_definition: person who competes in sports
example1: Olympic athletes train very hard daily.
example1_ru: Олимпийские спортсмены тренируются очень усердно каждый день.
example2: She is a professional athlete now.

en_definition: person or team that wins a competition
example1: He became the tennis champion this year.
example1_ru: В этом году он стал чемпионом по теннису.
example2: The champion defended their title.

en_definition: large competition with many teams or players
example1: The chess tournament starts next month.
example1_ru: Шахматный турнир начинается в следующем месяце.
example2: Many countries joined the tournament.

en_definition: person who plays on the same team
example1: My teammates are very supportive.
example1_ru: Мои товарищи по команде очень меня поддерживают.
example2: A good teammate helps everyone.

en_definition: success in a game or competition
example1: Their victory was well deserved.
example1_ru: Их победа была вполне заслуженной.
example2: The team celebrated their victory.

en_definition: loss in a game or competition
example1: They learned from their defeat.
example1_ru: Они извлекли урок из своего поражения.
example2: The defeat was disappointing for everyone.

en_definition: number of points or goals in sports
example1: The final score was ten to eight.
example1_ru: Итоговый счёт был десять восемь.
example2: What is the current score.

en_definition: to be successful in a competition
example1: She will win the game tomorrow.
example1_ru: Завтра она выиграет игру.
example2: Our team won the championship.

en_definition: to be unsuccessful in a competition
example1: We lost the game by one point.
example1_ru: Мы проиграли игру с разницей в одно очко.
example2: They never lose to this team.

en_definition: to try to be better than others
example1: Many countries compete in the Olympics.
example1_ru: Многие страны соревнуются на Олимпийских играх.
example2: She competes in swimming competitions.

en_definition: to exercise regularly to improve skills
example1: Athletes train every day to stay fit.
example1_ru: Спортсмены тренируются каждый день, чтобы поддерживать форму.
example2: I train at the gym three times weekly.

en_definition: activity you enjoy doing in free time
example1: My hobby is collecting vintage stamps.
example1_ru: Моё хобби — коллекционировать старинные марки.
example2: Photography is her favorite hobby.

en_definition: to rest and stop thinking about problems
example1: I like to relax by reading books.
example1_ru: Я люблю расслабляться за чтением книг.
example2: Take time to relax after work.

en_definition: to spend time with friends doing nothing
example1: We hung out at the mall today.
example1_ru: Сегодня мы тусовались в торговом центре.
example2: Let's hang out this weekend.

en_definition: activity or performance to amuse people
example1: The entertainment was excellent at the party.
example1_ru: Развлекательная программа на вечеринке была отличной.
example2: Live entertainment will be available.

en_definition: performance of music by musicians
example1: I went to a rock concert yesterday.
example1_ru: Вчера я ходил на рок-концерт.
example2: The concert was sold out completely.

en_definition: public display of art or interesting things
example1: The art exhibition opens next week.
example1_ru: Выставка искусства открывается на следующей неделе.
example2: I attended an interesting exhibition.

en_definition: picture made using colors and paint
example1: She created a beautiful painting.
example1_ru: Она создала прекрасную картину.
example2: The museum has many paintings.

en_definition: artwork made from stone or metal
example1: The sculpture was made of marble.
example1_ru: Скульптура была сделана из мрамора.
example2: Beautiful sculptures decorated the garden.

en_definition: serious play or performance on stage
example1: I enjoyed the drama performance.
example1_ru: Мне понравился этот драматический спектакль.
example2: She studies drama at university.

en_definition: funny play or movie that makes you laugh
example1: That comedy show was hilarious.
example1_ru: Это комедийное шоу было уморительным.
example2: I prefer comedies to serious movies.

en_definition: person who writes books or stories
example1: The author wrote an excellent novel.
example1_ru: Автор написал отличный роман.
example2: I met the author at the book signing.

en_definition: long fictional story written as a book
example1: I finished reading the novel yesterday.
example1_ru: Вчера я дочитал этот роман.
example2: The novel was written last century.

en_definition: type of literature with rhythm and emotion
example1: She enjoys writing poetry in her spare time.
example1_ru: В свободное время она любит писать стихи.
example2: Poetry can express deep feelings.

en_definition: building or room for displaying art
example1: We visited the art gallery yesterday.
example1_ru: Вчера мы посетили художественную галерею.
example2: The gallery displays modern paintings.

en_definition: building where historical objects are shown
example1: The museum has ancient artifacts.
example1_ru: В музее хранятся древние артефакты.
example2: We spent hours in the museum.

en_definition: theater where movies are shown
example1: Let's go to the cinema tonight.
example1_ru: Давай сегодня вечером сходим в кино.
example2: The cinema has comfortable seats.

en_definition: building where plays and performances happen
example1: The theatre is in the city center.
example1_ru: Театр находится в центре города.
example2: She works at the local theatre.

en_definition: to act or sing for an audience
example1: She performed beautifully on stage.
example1_ru: Она прекрасно выступила на сцене.
example2: They will perform at the concert.

en_definition: people who watch or listen to performance
example1: The audience applauded enthusiastically.
example1_ru: Публика с восторгом аплодировала.
example2: The audience was very large.

en_definition: special clothes worn for a role
example1: The actor wore an elaborate costume.
example1_ru: На актёре был замысловатый костюм.
example2: She designed her own costume.

en_definition: written text of a play or movie
example1: The actor memorized the script.
example1_ru: Актёр выучил сценарий наизусть.
example2: The script was very interesting.

en_definition: person in charge of making a film
example1: The director made an excellent movie.
example1_ru: Режиссёр снял отличный фильм.
example2: She is a talented film director.

en_definition: person who performs in plays or movies
example1: The main actor was very talented.
example1_ru: Главный актёр был очень талантлив.
example2: She wants to be an actor.

en_definition: place where someone is going
example1: Paris was our final destination.
example1_ru: Париж был нашим конечным пунктом назначения.
example2: What is your travel destination.

en_definition: act of traveling from one place to another
example1: The journey took six hours.
example1_ru: Поездка заняла шесть часов.
example2: We enjoyed our journey across Europe.

en_definition: planned route or program of travel
example1: The itinerary includes five cities.
example1_ru: Маршрут поездки включает пять городов.
example2: We follow a strict itinerary.

en_definition: person who shows travelers around places
example1: Our guide knew everything about history.
example1_ru: Наш гид знал всё об истории.
example2: The tour guide was very informative.

en_definition: vacation place with good entertainment
example1: We stayed at a beach resort.
example1_ru: Мы остановились на пляжном курорте.
example2: The ski resort is very popular.

en_definition: small gift to remember a place
example1: I bought souvenirs for my family.
example1_ru: Я купил сувениры для своей семьи.
example2: This souvenir reminds me of my trip.

en_definition: government officials checking travelers
example1: We waited in customs for two hours.
example1_ru: Мы прождали на таможне два часа.
example2: Customs inspection was quick.

en_definition: measures to keep people safe
example1: Security checked our bags carefully.
example1_ru: Служба безопасности тщательно проверила наши сумки.
example2: Airport security is very strict.

en_definition: act of leaving a place to travel
example1: The departure time is in one hour.
example1_ru: Время отправления — через час.
example2: Our departure is at 7am.

en_definition: act of reaching a destination
example1: Our arrival time is midnight.
example1_ru: Время нашего прибытия — полночь.
example2: The arrival hall was crowded.

en_definition: a longer route used instead of the usual road, often to avoid a problem
example1: The driver took a detour because the main road was closed.
example1_ru: Водитель поехал в объезд, потому что главная дорога была закрыта.
example2: Our detour through the hills added an extra hour to the trip.

en_definition: flight or train linking two places
example1: My connection is in an hour.
example1_ru: Моя пересадка через час.
example2: We missed our connection.

en_definition: paper showing you have paid for travel
example1: I bought a ticket for the train.
example1_ru: Я купил билет на поезд.
example2: The ticket was very expensive.

en_definition: booking something in advance
example1: I have a reservation at the hotel.
example1_ru: У меня забронирован номер в отеле.
example2: The reservation was confirmed.

en_definition: to visit a place or person for a short time, often without planning ahead
example1: Feel free to drop by our office whenever you are nearby.
example1_ru: Заходите к нам в офис в любое время, если будете поблизости.
example2: She will drop by the hotel to leave the keys with reception.

en_definition: to leave someone or something at a place
example1: Can you drop me off at the station.
example1_ru: Ты можешь высадить меня у вокзала?
example2: I will drop off my bags.

en_definition: person traveling for pleasure
example1: Many tourists visit Paris yearly.
example1_ru: Много туристов посещают Париж ежегодно.
example2: Tourists often buy souvenirs.

en_definition: a piece of paper money issued by a bank or government
example1: He handed the cashier a banknote and waited for change.
example1_ru: Он протянул кассиру купюру и стал ждать сдачу.
example2: The old banknote was no longer accepted at the store.

en_definition: a machine outside a bank that lets you withdraw money with a card
example1: We found a cash machine near the train station.
example1_ru: Мы нашли банкомат возле вокзала.
example2: The cash machine at the airport charges an extra fee.

en_definition: a printed check that travelers buy and can exchange for cash abroad
example1: Fewer tourists use a traveler's check now that cards are common.
example1_ru: Теперь, когда карты стали привычным делом, дорожными чеками пользуется всё меньше туристов.
example2: She kept her traveler's check safe inside a zipped pocket.

en_definition: money spent on something
example1: My travel expenses were high.
example1_ru: Мои расходы на поездку были высокими.
example2: We kept track of expenses.

en_definition: famous building or place in a city
example1: The Eiffel Tower is a famous landmark.
example1_ru: Эйфелева башня — знаменитая достопримечательность.
example2: We visited many landmarks.

en_definition: having beautiful views of nature
example1: The route has scenic mountains.
example1_ru: На маршруте живописные горы.
example2: That scenic view was amazing.

en_definition: involving possibility of danger
example1: That activity seemed quite risky.
example1_ru: Это занятие показалось довольно рискованным.
example2: Risky behavior should be avoided.

en_definition: careful to avoid danger or problems
example1: A cautious person thinks before acting.
example1_ru: Осторожный человек думает, прежде чем действовать.
example2: Be cautious when driving.

en_definition: willing to take risks and try new things
example1: She has an adventurous spirit.
example1_ru: У неё авантюрный дух.
example2: Adventurous travelers explore more.
